    • Advanced count time-series uncertainty and forecast evaluation

Rolling-origin backtesting for count forecasts

Source: R/forecast_uncertainty.R
rolling_origin_evaluate.Rd

Runs rolling-origin forecast evaluation by repeatedly refitting on expanding windows and scoring out-of-sample forecast horizons. The default rt_method = "growth" maps growth-rate uncertainty from fit_poisson_growth() into R_t draws used by simulate_renewal_forecast().

Usage

rolling_origin_evaluate(
  data,
  date_col = "date",
  count_col = "incidence",
  initial_window,
  horizon = 7L,
  step = 1L,
  serial_interval,
  rt_method = c("growth", "fixed"),
  fixed_rt = 1,
  n_sims = 500L,
  mean_si = 4.7,
  interval_level = 0.9,
  wis_levels = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95),
  seed = NULL
)

Arguments

data

Data frame containing date and incidence columns.

date_col

Name of date column in data.

count_col

Name of incidence column in data.

initial_window

Initial training-window length.

horizon

Forecast horizon for each origin.

step

Step size between forecast origins.

serial_interval

Numeric serial interval probability vector.

rt_method

Method for generating R_t forecasts ("growth" or "fixed").

fixed_rt

Scalar or length-horizon vector of fixed R_t values when rt_method = "fixed".

n_sims

Number of forecast simulations per origin.

mean_si

Mean serial interval used to map growth-rate draws to R_t draws under rt_method = "growth".

interval_level

Central interval level for coverage in scoring.

wis_levels

Levels used for WIS.

seed

Optional random seed.

Value

A list with components:

  • scores: day-level scores across all origins,

  • origin_summary: origin-level aggregated metrics,

  • overall: global aggregated metrics,

  • forecast_summary: predictive summary quantiles by origin/day.
